title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, with this easy-to-follow recipe. Perfect for a delicious appetizer or meal!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Haemul Pajeon with shrimp and squid
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients for Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)
How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Instructions
Tips for the Perfect Crispy Haemul Pajeon
Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce for Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon, an authentic Korean seafood pancake with shrimp, squid, and green onions, served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a homemade Haemul Pajeon, a savory and crispy Korean seafood pancake, perfect for sharing!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake loaded with an abundance of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Its crispy exterior and tender, flavorful interior make it a perfect appetizer or light meal, especially when paired with a tangy dipping sauce. This traditional dish offers a delightful taste of Korean culinary heritage.

instructions.title: Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly clean and prepare all seafood. Chop shrimp and squid into bite-sized pieces. If using mussels or clams, ensure they are cleaned and shelled, then chop. Wash and trim green onions, cutting them into 3-4 inch segments. Thinly slice the yellow onion and red chili pepper.

instructions.title: Mix the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, salt, and beaten egg. Gradually add the cold water, whisking until a smooth batter forms. The batter should be slightly thick, similar to a crepe batter. Avoid overmixing to prevent a tough pancake.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and toasted sesame seeds. If desired, add red pepper flakes for a spicy kick. Stir well and set aside.

instructions.title: Combine Batter with Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Add the prepared shrimp, squid, mussels/clams (if using), and sliced yellow onion to the pancake batter. Gently fold to combine, ensuring the seafood and onion are evenly coated. Reserve the green onion segments for layering.

instructions.title: Cook the First Haemul Pajeon
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick pan or cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake. Immediately arrange half of the green onion segments on top of the batter, pressing them gently into the pancake. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy. Add a drizzle of oil around the edges, then carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 5-7 minutes until the other side is golden and crispy, and the seafood is cooked through.

instructions.title: Cook the Second Haemul Pajeon and Serve
instructions.text: Slide the first cooked Haemul Pajeon onto a serving plate. Repeat the cooking process with the remaining oil, batter, and green onions to make the second pancake. Once both are cooked, slice them into wedges, garnish with sliced red chili pepper if using, and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce. Enjoy the crispy, savory goodness!

tips: For an extra crispy Pajeon, use very cold water for the batter and add a tablespoon of rice flour or glutinous rice flour along with the all-purpose flour and cornstarch.
Do not overcrowd the pan. Cook one pancake at a time to ensure even cooking and crispiness. A hot pan is crucial for a perfectly golden crust.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its best texture. It pairs wonderfully with Korean makgeolli (rice wine) or a cold beer.
article:
Discover the culinary delight that is Haemul Pajeon, a quintessential Korean seafood pancake cherished for its crispy edges, tender interior, and a generous medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This savory dish is more than just a pancake; it's a celebration of flavors and textures, deeply rooted in Korean food culture, often enjoyed on rainy days with a side of makgeolli (Korean rice wine). If you've ever craved a dish that perfectly balances comfort with an exciting burst of umami, Haemul Pajeon is an absolute must-try. This article will guide you through everything you need to know about this beloved Korean delicacy, from its origins and ingredients to a step-by-step recipe and expert tips for achieving the ultimate crispy perfection.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

Haemul Pajeon translates literally to "seafood scallion pancake," and it’s a dish that lives up to its name with delicious accuracy. Unlike the sweet pancakes many Westerners are accustomed to, pajeon is a savory affair, serving as a popular appetizer, snack, or even a light meal in Korea. The base is a simple batter, typically made from wheat flour, rice flour, or a combination, mixed with water and eggs to create a pourable consistency. What truly sets Haemul Pajeon apart is its abundant use of fresh scallions, laid out like green planks across the pancake, and a colorful array of seafood. Common seafood additions include shrimp, squid, mussels, and oysters, but the beauty lies in its versatility, allowing for whatever fresh catch is available. The pancake is pan-fried until golden brown and crispy on the outside, while remaining delightfully soft and flavorful within. It’s often served hot, cut into bite-sized wedges, alongside a tangy, savory dipping sauce.

## Ingredients for Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)

To embark on your Haemul Pajeon journey, gathering the right ingredients is key. Here’s a comprehensive list for a classic rendition, serving 2-4 people:

For the Batter:
*   1 cup (120g) all-purpose flour
*   1/2 cup (60g) rice flour (optional, but adds crispiness)
*   1 large egg
*   1 1/2 cups cold water (or anchovy broth for deeper flavor)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt

For the Fillings:
*   1 bunch (about 10-12) green onions (scallions), trimmed and cut into 3-4 inch pieces
*   8 oz (225g) mixed seafood (e.g., shrimp, squid, mussels, oysters), cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces
*   1/2 small onion, thinly sliced (optional)
*   1/2 small red bell pepper, thinly sliced (optional, for color and slight sweetness)
*   1-2 fresh red or green chilies, thinly sliced (optional, for heat)

For Frying:
*   Vegetable oil or neutral cooking oil

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) or a pinch of red pepper flakes
*   1/2 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)

Ensure your seafood is fresh and properly cleaned. For the green onions, the thicker white and light green parts are preferred as they hold up well during cooking and offer a satisfying crunch.

## How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Instructions

Making a delicious Haemul Pajeon is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for a perfect outcome:

1.  **Prepare the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (if using), salt, egg, and cold water until smooth. Be careful not to overmix; a few small lumps are acceptable. Let the batter rest for 10-15 minutes while you prepare the other ingredients. This allows the gluten to relax, resulting in a more tender pancake.
2.  **Prepare Fillings:** Clean and pat dry your mixed seafood. Cut any larger pieces into bite-sized portions. Trim the green onions and cut them into 3-4 inch lengths. If using, thinly slice the onion, bell pepper, and chilies.
3.  **Combine Ingredients (Optional Method):** Some prefer to mix the seafood and green onions directly into the batter. However, for a more traditional and visually appealing Haemul Pajeon, we'll arrange them directly in the pan.
4.  **Heat the Pan:** Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of vegetable oil (about 2-3 tablespoons) to coat the bottom of the pan evenly. The oil should be hot but not smoking.
5.  **Arrange Green Onions and Seafood:** Once the oil is hot, carefully arrange a layer of green onion pieces in the pan, ensuring they cover most of the surface. Scatter your prepared seafood evenly over the green onions. If using, add sliced onions, bell peppers, and chilies at this stage.
6.  **Pour the Batter:** Gently pour about 1 cup of batter over the green onions and seafood, spreading it evenly to cover all the ingredients and form a round pancake. You should hear a satisfying sizzle.
7.  **Cook the First Side:** Cook for 4-6 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to set. You can carefully lift an edge with a spatula to check the color.
8.  **Flip and Cook the Second Side:** Add a little more oil around the edges of the pancake, if needed. Using a large, sturdy spatula (or two spatulas), carefully flip the pancake. Cook for another 3-5 minutes, pressing down gently with the spatula to ensure even contact with the pan, until the second side is golden brown and the seafood is cooked through.
9.  **Repeat:** Slide the cooked Haemul Pajeon onto a cutting board. Repeat the process with the remaining batter and fillings.
10. **Prepare Dipping Sauce:** While the pancakes cook, whisk together all the dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl.

## Tips for the Perfect Crispy Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that irresistible crispy exterior while keeping the inside tender is the hallmark of a great Haemul Pajeon. Here are some pro tips:

*   **Cold Water/Broth:** Always use cold water or broth for your batter. This helps prevent gluten development and keeps the batter light, contributing to a crispier pancake.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter:** Overmixing develops gluten, leading to a chewy rather than tender pancake. A few small lumps are perfectly fine.
*   **Generous Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil. Haemul Pajeon needs enough oil to properly fry and achieve that desirable crispiness. Think of it more like shallow frying than just pan-frying.
*   **Hot Pan, Medium-High Heat:** Start with a hot pan to get an immediate sizzle and crisping effect. Maintain medium-high heat throughout cooking to ensure the pancake cooks through without burning.
*   **Thin Layer of Batter:** While you want enough batter to bind the ingredients, a thin layer ensures that the green onions and seafood are the stars and allows for maximum crispness.
*   **Rest the Batter:** Letting the batter rest for 10-15 minutes allows the flour to fully hydrate and the gluten to relax, leading to a more tender texture.
*   **Press Down Gently:** After flipping, gently press down on the pancake with your spatula. This ensures even contact with the hot pan, promoting uniform browning and crispiness.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce for Pajeon

Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed immediately after cooking, while it’s still piping hot and at its crispiest. Cut the large pancake into wedge-shaped pieces or squares using a pizza cutter or a knife, making them easy to share and dip.

The accompanying dipping sauce is crucial for balancing the rich flavors of the pancake. The simple recipe provided earlier (soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, chili flakes, sesame seeds, and optional minced garlic) offers a perfect blend of salty, tangy, and subtly spicy notes that cut through the richness of the pancake.

In Korea, pajeon is a popular anju (drinking snack), especially paired with makgeolli. The milky, slightly sweet, and effervescent rice wine complements the savory pancake beautifully. You can also serve Haemul Pajeon alongside other Korean side dishes (banchan) like kimchi, pickled radishes, or seasoned bean sprouts for a more complete meal. For a truly authentic experience, enjoy it with friends or family, sharing stories as you share the delicious pancake.

## Haemul Pajeon Variations and Substitutions

The beauty of Haemul Pajeon lies in its adaptability. While the classic version is fantastic, don't hesitate to experiment with variations to suit your taste and what you have on hand.

*   **Seafood Choices:** Feel free to customize your seafood mix. Clams, octopus, or even imitation crab meat can be added. If fresh seafood isn't available, frozen mixed seafood works well – just be sure to thaw and pat it very dry to prevent a watery pancake.
*   **Vegetable Additions:** Beyond scallions, many enjoy adding other vegetables. Thinly sliced carrots, zucchini, mushrooms, or even a handful of finely chopped kimchi can introduce new flavors and textures. For kimchi pajeon, you'd typically mix the kimchi directly into the batter.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan Option:** To make a vegetarian version (Yachaejeon), simply omit the seafood and increase the amount of vegetables, perhaps adding more mushrooms, onions, and bell peppers. For a vegan version, substitute the egg in the batter with a flax egg (1 tbsp ground flaxseed + 3 tbsp water, let sit for 5 mins) or a commercial egg replacer, and ensure your broth is vegetable-based.
*   **Different Flours:** While all-purpose and rice flour are standard, some recipes incorporate a small amount of cornstarch for extra crispiness or potato starch for a chewier texture. You can also find pre-made Korean pancake mixes at Asian grocery stores, which often contain a blend of flours and seasonings.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the spice by adding more or fewer fresh chilies to the pancake itself, or by increasing the gochugaru in the dipping sauce.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just food; it's an experience. Its comforting warmth, delightful crispiness, and savory seafood flavors make it a beloved dish for many. Whether you're a seasoned Korean food enthusiast or a curious newcomer, making this iconic seafood pancake at home is a rewarding culinary adventure. So, grab your ingredients, fire up your pan, and get ready to enjoy a taste of Korea right in your own kitchen!
